text: **Refinement of the critique on "durable input journal as first writer":** the critique wants the journal as first writer *everywhere*. I disagree — make it first writer only on **high-value, promotion-target seams** (turn lifecycle, cursor, outbox loop), not on every store mutation. The journal earns its cost where the surface is being promoted and where split-brain is a real risk; forcing every incidental DB write through a fact is charter-violating ceremony (bulk payloads, clone cost) with no leadership payoff. Capsules give reproduce-ability on the surfaces that matter; the seam map is honest about the rest being uncovered.
